message:
fix(command): harden Lua table library functions with proper type checking and bounds validation

Add lua_table_arg() helper that extracts table references with proper
error messages, eliminating silent no-ops when non-table values are
passed to table.insert, table.remove, and table.concat.

Fix table.insert to reject out-of-bounds positions instead of silently
clamping — matching Lua 5.4 semantics where position must be in
[1, #t+1]. Fix table.remove to validate its first argument is a table
before attempting mutation.

Simplify table.concat by using the new lua_table_arg helper.

Also includes store improvements and new conformance test fixtures
for core scripting operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

     },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_concat_rejects_non_table_argument",
+      "now_ms": 100,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "return table.concat(42, '-')",
+        "0"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "error",
+        "value": "ERR Error running script: bad argument #1 to 'concat' (table expected, got number)"
+      }
+    },
     {
       "name": "eval_select_function",
       "now_ms": 100,

+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "error",
+        "value": "ERR Error running script: bad argument #2 to 'insert' (position out of bounds)"
+      }
